questions.text: If two axes are of equal length and the third is either shorter or longer than the other two, all angles are 90˚.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: In a cubic system, all three axes are of equal length and all are at right angles to each other.
In a tetragonal system, two axes are equal in length and the third axes are either shorter or longer than the other two. All angles are 90°.
In a triclinic system, all three axes and three angles are unequal and none of the angles is 90°.

In a hexagonal system, two axes are of equal length and are in one plane making an angle of 120° with each other. The third axis which is different in length than the other two is at a right angle to these two axes.

questions.explanation: Option A: In a cubic system, all three axes are of equal length and all are at right angles to each other. Option: B In a tetragonal system, two axes are equal in length and the third axes are either shorter or longer than the other two. All angles are 90°. Option C: In a triclinic system, all three axes and three angles are unequal and none of the angles is 90°. Option D: In a hexagonal system, two axes are of equal length and are in one plane making an angle of 120 ° with each other. The third axis which is different in length than the other two is at a right angle to these two axes.

questions.text: Angles in a unit cell are denoted by _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Angles are determined by alpha, beta, and gamma.
Angles are determined by alpha, beta, and gamma.

In a unit cell, two different parameters are defined by different notations. 1: Length in a unit cell is determined by x, y and z. 2: Angles are determined by alpha, beta and gamma. Thus, marking Option C as correct.
Angles are determined by alpha, beta, and gamma.

questions.explanation: In a unit cell, two different parameters are defined by different notations. 1 Length in a unit cell is determined by x, y and z. 2: Angles are determined by alpha, beta and gamma. Thus, marking Option C as correct.

questions.text: The Unit cell with crystallographic dimensions a=b≠c , α=β=γ=90 degrees is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is not correct. A cubic unit cell has three equal dimensions (a = b = c) and all angles equal to 90 degrees (α = β = γ = 90°).
A tetragonal unit cell has two equal dimensions (a = b ≠ c) and all angles equal to 90 degrees (α = β = γ = 90°).
This option is also incorrect. A Monoclinic unit cell has three unequal dimensions (a ≠ b ≠ c) and one angle (β) that is not equal to 90 degrees (α = γ = 90°).

This option is also incorrect. A hexagonal unit cell has two equal dimensions (a = b ≠ c) and all angles equal to 90 degrees (α = β = 90°) but has a third axis that is perpendicular to the plane of the other two axes and is longer than them.

questions.explanation: A tetragonal unit cell has two equal dimensions (a = b ≠ c) and all angles equal to 90 degrees (α = β = γ = 90°).

questions.text: The unit cell parameters of the monoclinic system are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This describes a tetragonal system where two axes are equal, and all angles are right angles.
This correctly describes the monoclinic system, where all axes are unequal, and only two angles are right angles.
This represents an orthorhombic system, not hexagonal, where all angles are right angles but sides are unequal.
This describes a cubic system where all sides and angles are equal.

questions.explanation: The monoclinic crystal system is characterized by three unequal axes, with two of them being perpendicular (α=γ=90°) and the third angle (β) differing from 90°. This distinguishes the monoclinic system from others where either all axes are equal or all angles are right angles. Option B correctly represents this with a≠b≠c ; α=γ=90° ; β≠90°. Options A, C, and D describe tetragonal, orthorhombic, and cubic systems, respectively, which do not fit the monoclinic parameters.

questions.text: ABCABC... type of arrangement of different layers, in a closest packed structure is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: ABCABC is indeed not associated with an HCP (Hexagonal Close-Packed) structure. The HCP structure follows a different stacking sequence with ABAB, resulting in a hexagonal pattern when viewed from the top. In an HCP structure, there are two layers of atoms or ions in the unit cell, and the arrangement is hexagonal rather than cubic.

The sequence ABCABC is not a characteristic of a body-centered cubic (BCC) structure. The BCC structure has a different layering arrangement compared to the ABCABC sequence. In a BCC structure, the layers follow an AABBCC... sequence.

The sequence ABCABC typically does not describe a simple cubic (SC) structure. Simple cubic (SC) structure has a different arrangement where there is one atom at each corner of a cube, and the layers are not packed as densely as in an HCP structure. The SC structure is much simpler and has a lower packing efficiency compared to HCP and other closely packed structures.

In a CCP structure, the layers of atoms or ions are indeed stacked in a manner where each layer is positioned directly above the preceding layer, following the sequence ABCABC. This results in a cubic pattern when viewed from the top. This arrangement is commonly found in many metallic crystal structures, including those of elements like copper and aluminum.

questions.explanation: ABCABC... layer arrangement means the layers repeat in the sequence A, B, C, then again A, B, C.This stacking sequence corresponds to Cubic Close Packing (CCP), also known as Face-Centered Cubic (FCC) structure.Hexagonal Close Packing (HCP) has an ABAB... stacking pattern.Body-centered Cubic (BCC) and Simple Cubic are not close-packed structures and have different arrangements.

questions.text: The crystal system having a = β ≠ c and α = β = γ = 90° is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The hexagonal system consists of four axes: three equal axes at 120-degree angles and a vertical axis perpendicular to them. This does not match the condition a = β ≠ c.
In the Tetragonal system, two axes (a and b) are of equal length, and the third axis (c) differs in length, with all angles being 90°. This matches the given condition a = β ≠ c and α = β = γ = 90°.
The Monoclinic system has all axes of different lengths, and only two angles are 90°. The angle β is not 90°, which does not satisfy the given condition α = β = γ = 90°.
In the Triclinic system, all axes have different lengths, and none of the angles are 90°. This does not satisfy the given condition of all angles being 90°.

questions.explanation: In the Tetragonal crystal system:The three crystallographic axes are all perpendicular to each other (α = β = γ = 90°).Two of the axes (a and b) have the same length (a = b), and these are perpendicular to each other.The third axis (c) has a different length (c ≠ a or b). So, in the Tetragonal crystal system, the unit cell is a rectangular prism with two edges of equal length (a and b) and one edge of a different length (c), with all right angles between the edges.

questions.text: In crystalline structure, when the length of all axes and angles are unequal then crystal will be.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: All axes are of equal length, and all angles are 90°.
Two axes are of unequal length, and one angle is not 90°, while the other two are.
All axes are of unequal length, but all angles are 90°.

In crystallography, the crystal system is determined by the lengths of the axes and the angles between them. The triclinic crystal system is characterized by:- All axes (a, b, and c) are of unequal length- All angles (α, β, and γ) are of unequal measure and not equal to 90°This means that the crystal has no symmetry or repeating pattern, and the axes are not perpendicular to each other.

questions.explanation: In crystallography, the crystal system is determined by the lengths of the axes and the angles between them. The triclinic crystal system is characterized by:- All axes (a, b, and c) are of unequal length- All angles (α, β, and γ) are of unequal measure and not equal to 90°This means that the crystal has no symmetry or repeating pattern, and the axes are not perpendicular to each other.

questions.text: The shape of the crystal in which it usually grows is called its _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of size.
This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of capacity.
This option is correct because it is a definition of habit. Habit of a crystal is defined as the shape of the crystal in which it usually grows.
This option is incorrect because it is not a correct definition of property.

questions.explanation: The correct option is C. The Habit of a crystal is defined as the shape of the crystal in which it usually grows.

questions.text: Structure of ice is similar to which of the following?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Liquid water does not have a regular repeating pattern, and the arrangement of water molecules is more disordered compared to the solid state.

The structure of ice is similar to the structure of DIAMOND. Ice and diamond are both examples of crystalline structures. In ice, water molecules are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern forming a crystal lattice. Each water molecule is bonded to four neighboring water molecules through hydrogen bonds. This arrangement gives ice its solid and stable structure. Similarly, a diamond is also a crystalline structure, where carbon atoms are arranged in a three-dimensional lattice. Each carbon atom is covalently bonded to four other carbon atoms, creating a very rigid and stable structure.
Graphite is composed of carbon atoms arranged in layers.
Sucrose is a molecular compound with a different structure altogether.

questions.explanation: The correct option is B. The structure of ice is similar to the structure of DIAMOND. Ice and diamond are both examples of crystalline structures. In ice, water molecules are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern forming a crystal lattice. Each water molecule is bonded to four neighboring water molecules through hydrogen bonds. This arrangement gives ice its solid and stable structure. Similarly, a diamond is also a crystalline structure, where carbon atoms are arranged in a three-dimensional lattice. Each carbon atom is covalently bonded to four other carbon atoms, creating a very rigid and stable structure.

questions.text: Which of the following solids is isotropic?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because Ionic solids are composed of cations and anions held together by electrostatic forces. Due to the strength of these interactions, ionic solids tend to be hard, brittle, and have high melting points.

This option is incorrect because Molecular solids are composed of discrete molecules held together by intermolecular forces. Because these interactions are relatively weak, molecular solids tend to be soft and have low to moderate melting points.
This option is correct because Amorphous solids are isotropic. That is, they exhibit uniform properties in all directions.

This option is incorrect because Metallic solids are materials that consist of metal atoms exclusively, giving them a unique set of properties. They are held together by a special type of bond called metallic bonding. This occurs when a lattice of positively charged metal ions is surrounded by a sea of delocalized electrons.

questions.explanation: The correct option is C. Amorphous solids are isotropic. That is, they exhibit uniform properties in all directions. The thermal and electrical conductivities, coefficient of thermal expansion, and refractive index of an amorphous solid have the same value in whatever direction the properties are measured.

questions.text: NaCl and CsF have same geometry because they have the same _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionisation energy refers to the amount of energy required to remove an electron from an atom or a positive ion. It is a measure of how strongly an atom holds onto its electrons.When an electron is removed from an atom, it creates a positively charged ion. The ionisation energy is the energy needed to overcome the attractive forces between the positively charged nucleus and the negatively charged electron, allowing the electron to be removed.Ionisation energy values vary for different elements and atoms. Generally, elements with higher atomic numbers tend to have higher ionisation energies because the electrons are held more tightly due to increased nuclear charge. Additionally, the ionisation energy generally increases as you move across a period in the periodic table from left to right.Ionisation energy is an important property as it helps us understand the reactivity and behavior of elements. Elements with low ionisation energies tend to be more reactive, as they can easily lose electrons and form positive ions.

NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure.

Electron affinity refers to the energy change that occurs when an atom or molecule gains an electron to form a negative ion. It is a measure of how much an atom or molecule "wants" or attracts an additional electron.When an atom or molecule gains an electron, it goes from a neutral state to a negatively charged state. The electron affinity is the energy released or absorbed during this process. If the electron affinity is positive, it means that energy is released when an electron is added, indicating a favorable electron gain. On the other hand, if the electron affinity is negative, it means that energy is required to add an electron, indicating an unfavorable electron gain.The electron affinity of an element or molecule depends on various factors such as its atomic or molecular structure, the number of electrons already present, and the electronic configuration. Elements with higher electron affinities tend to have a greater tendency to gain electrons and form negative ions.

Incorrect option!NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure.

questions.explanation: NaCl and CsF have the same geometry because they have the same ionic radius ratio. The ionic radius ratio is the ratio of the radius of the cation to the radius of the anion in an ionic compound. In both NaCl and CsF, the cation is smaller than the anion, but the ratio of their radii is the same. As a result, both compounds adopt the same crystal structure, which is face-centered cubic. The other properties mentioned, such as ionization energy, electron affinity, and bond length, may be different for NaCl and CsF because they have different cations and anions. However, these properties do not directly affect the geometry of the crystal structure

questions.text: Crystals are obtained by the saturated solution through?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Sedimentation is the process of particles settling to the bottom of a liquid. This process is used to separate solids from liquids, but it does not produce crystals.
Drying is the process of removing water or other solvents from a substance. This process can be used to obtain crystals, but it is not the only step involved.
Heating can be used to dissolve solids in solvents, but it does not produce crystals.

Crystals are obtained from a saturated solution through cooling. When a saturated solution is cooled, the solute molecules can no longer stay dissolved in the solvent and they start to form crystals. The crystals will continue to grow until the solution is no longer saturated.

questions.explanation: Crystals are obtained from a saturated solution through cooling. When a saturated solution is cooled, the solute molecules can no longer stay dissolved in the solvent and they start to form crystals. The crystals will continue to grow until the solution is no longer saturated.

questions.text: Crystalline solids are made up of _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of atoms. Crystalline solids can consist of various types of particles, including atoms, molecules, or ions. Examples of crystalline solids made up of atoms include diamond (composed of carbon atoms) and metallic crystals (composed of metal atoms).

This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of molecules. Crystalline solids can contain molecules as their building blocks, such as sugar (composed of individual sugar molecules) or ice (composed of water molecules). However, many other crystalline solids, like sodium chloride (NaCl) or calcium carbonate (CaCO3), are not composed of discrete molecules but instead consist of ions or atoms.

This option is incorrect because not all crystalline solids are made up of ions. Some crystalline solids, like molecular crystals, are composed of molecules rather than ions. However, many ionic compounds form crystalline solids, such as sodium chloride (NaCl) or potassium nitrate (KNO3), where the crystal lattice is made up of positively and negatively charged ions held together by ionic bonds.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: The correct answer is "all of these," because crystalline solids can be made up of a variety of constituents, including atoms, molecules, and ions. Crystalline solids are characterized by their highly ordered and repeating three-dimensional arrangements of constituent particles. These particles can be atoms, molecules, or ions depending on the specific substance.

questions.explanation: Crystalline solids are a three-dimensional collection of individual atoms, ions, or whole molecules organized in repeating patterns.

questions.text: The existence of an element in more than one crystalline form is called
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isomerism refers to the phenomenon in which two or more compounds have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements.
Allotropy is the existence of two or more different forms of an element in the same physical state.
Anisotropy refers to the property of a material or a system that exhibits different physical or mechanical properties when measured along different axis or directions.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: Only option b is correct, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Allotropy, or allotropism, is the property of some elements to exist in two or more different forms in the same physical state, known as allotropes of the elements. Allotropes are different structural modifications of an element the atoms of the element are bonded together differently. Examples are diamond and graphite, which are both allotropic forms of carbon.

questions.text: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes to other is called _ temperature:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Solution and Colloids

options.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.explanation: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes into another is called the transition temperature or phase transition temperature. This process is commonly observed in materials undergoing a phase transition, such as from a solid to another solid polymorphic form. The specific terms for these transitions can vary depending on the nature of the substance, for example, it could be called a melting point, freezing point, or transition temperature.

questions.text: Melting point of crystalline solids are
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Option A is correct since crystalline solids have a sharp melting point which is fixed. This feature is a characteristic of crystalline solids.
Option B is incorrect since it shows the characteristics of amorphous solids.
Option C is incorrect since it shows that the crystalline solid is impure.
Option D is incorrect since it states none of these which is not the case.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct since crystalline solids have a sharp melting point which is fixed. This feature is a characteristic of crystalline solids. Option B is incorrect since it shows the characteristics of amorphous solids. Option C is incorrect since it shows that the crystalline solid is impure. Option D is incorrect since it states none of these which is not the case.

questions.text: Structure of a crystal is changed due to ---------
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

Incorrect option. Impurities change growth rates, shape, and thickness of growth layers and growth habits, and are trapped as impurity striations and large clusters of impurities and inhomogeneities. Some of these effects of impurities are reviewed for the growth of bulk single crystals.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since the presence of impurity due to its different electronic structure produces disturbance in the conduction electron distribution, distortion of the crystal lattice, and polarization of electronic charge. These facts change the various electronic properties of metallic solids significantly.

questions.text: 'Anisotropy' is a property of crystal based on its -------
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Chemical properties refer to how a substance interacts with other substances to form new chemical compounds.

A lattice refers to a regular and repeating three-dimensional pattern of atoms, ions, or molecules that make up a crystal structure. The lattice structure can be thought of as a three-dimensional network of points, where each point represents an atom, ion, or molecule.
Physical properties refer to characteristics that can be observed or measured without changing the substance's chemical identity, such as color, melting point, boiling point, etc.
Density is a physical property that describes how much mass is contained in a given volume of a substance.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct since some crystals show variation in physical properties depending upon their direction of cleavage. Such properties are called 'Anisotropic Properties'. For example: refractive index, and electric and thermal conductivities.

questions.text: Which one of the following can conduct electricity in a solid state?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Diamond does not conduct electricity.
In graphite each carbon is bonded to three other carbon atoms hence leaving behind a free electron. Due to the presence of this one delocalised electron, graphite is a good conductor of electricity .

Fingernails do not conduct electricity and thus cannot be employed to enter information on a conductive touch screen. Users with long fingernails cannot easily touch the touch screen with their finger.
Iodine is a nonmetal and therefore, it is an insulator that is, relatively, a poor conductor of electricity and heat .

questions.explanation: Option A: Diamond does not conduct electricity.Option B: In graphite, each carbon is bonded to three other carbon atoms hence leaving behind a free electron. Due to the presence of this one delocalized electron, graphite is a good conductor of electricity.Option C: fingernails do not conduct electricity and thus cannot be employed to enter information on a conductive touch screen. Users with long fingernails cannot easily touch the touch screen with their fingerOption D: Iodine is a nonmetal and therefore, it is an insulator that is, relatively, a poor conductor of electricity and heat.

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect since cooling affects the decrease in temperature.
Option B is incorrect since heating causes the bonds between the atoms to relax.
Addition of impurity in a crystal alters it structure, thus physical properties are also changed.

questions.text: Diamond and Graphite
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isotopes can be defined as the variants of chemical elements that possess the same number of protons and electrons, but a different number of neutrons. Both allotropes and isotopes refer to different forms of a particular chemical element. Allotropes explain the differences in molecular structures. Isotopes explain the differences in atomic structures. Diamond and graphite have differences in their molecular structures, not atomic so option A is incorrect.

Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers. The difference in arrangement of carbon atoms is due to allotropy not isomerism so option B is incorrect.

Diamond and Graphite are both allotropes of carbon. Allotropes of some chemical elements exist in two or more different forms, in the same physical state. Allotropes are different structural modifications of an element: the atoms of the element are bonded together differently. Allotropes may show differences in chemical and physical properties.
Allotropes of an element have different structures so option D in incorrect.
Graphite is very soft and has a hardness of 1 to 2 on the Mohs scale. Diamonds are the hardest known natural substance and has a hardness of 10 so option E is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Diamond and Graphite are allotropes of carbon. The term allotrope refers to one or more physical forms of a chemical element that occurs in the same physical state. Allotropes may show differences in chemical and physical properties.

questions.text: When a compound exists in more than one crystalline form, the phenomenon is termed as:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: When two compounds exist in the same crystalline form, it is called isomorphism.
Correct. When a compound exists in more than one crystalline form, the phenomenon is termed as polymorphism.
When two compunds have same molecular formula but different structure formula, the phenomenon is called isomerism.
The quality of exhibiting properties with different values when measured along axes in different directions is called anisotropy.
Enthalpy is energy change in a system.

questions.explanation: The phenomenon of a compound existing in more than one crystalline form is known as polymorphism. Polymorphism refers to the ability of a substance to exist in multiple crystal structures or forms while maintaining the same chemical composition. Different crystalline forms, also called polymorphs, may have distinct physical properties such as density, melting point, and optical properties. This phenomenon is observed in various substances, including elements, compounds, and minerals.

questions.text: Electrical conductivity of graphite is greater in one direction than in another due to
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isomorphism refers to the resemblance of in the crystalline structure of to different compounds.
A cleavage plane is the fracture of a crystal or metal by crack propagation across a crystallographic plane or cleavage plane.

Anisotropy means having a physical property that has a different value when measured in different directionsGraphite is a crystalline, anisotropic solid. Graphite can conduct electricity parallel to the planes of a hexagon but its insulator conducts electricity in a direction that is perpendicular to the planes.
Molecular symmetry in chemistry describes the symmetry present in molecules and the classification of these molecules according to their symmetry.

questions.explanation: Anisotropy means having a physical property that has a different value when measured in different directions Graphite is a crystalline, anisotropic solid. Graphite can conduct electricity parallel to the planes of a hexagon but its insulator conducts electricity in a direction that is perpendicular to the planes.

questions.text: Which of the following is isotropic property?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The refractive index varies with direction in anisotropic materials such as crystals, making it an anisotropic property.
In anisotropic materials like crystals, the coefficient of thermal expansion changes with direction, indicating it's not isotropic.
Cleavage involves breaking along specific planes in crystals, which varies with direction, thus being anisotropic.
Metallic conduction does not depend on direction; electrons move freely in all directions, making it an isotropic property.

questions.explanation: Metallic conduction (Option D) is an example of an isotropic property. Isotropic properties are those that exhibit the same value or behavior in all directions. In metallic conduction, the movement of electrons through a metal is not affected by the direction in which they travel within the material. Electrons move freely and uniformly in all directions within a metal, making metallic conduction an isotropic property.

questions.text: Which one has the highest melting point and boiling point?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic crystals have high melting and boiling points due to strong electrostatic forces between oppositely charged ions. However, covalent crystals have stronger directional covalent bonds that require even more energy to break.

Molecular crystals consist of molecules held together by weak intermolecular forces such as Van der Waals forces, resulting in relatively low melting and boiling points compared to other types of crystals.

Covalent crystals are characterized by strong covalent bonds in a rigid lattice, requiring substantial energy to break these bonds. This results in very high melting and boiling points, often exceeding those of ionic crystals.

Metallic crystals have metallic bonds, which can vary in strength. While they generally have high melting and boiling points, they often do not exceed those of covalent crystals, which have stronger covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: Covalent crystals, due to the strong covalent bonds holding their structures together, typically exhibit the highest melting and boiling points among the given options. Therefore, option c is correct.

questions.text: Same element existing in different shape is called
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Allotropy refers to the phenomenon where the same chemical element exists in different structural forms in the same physical state.
Isomorphism refers to the existence of two or more compounds with the same crystal structure, but not necessarily the same element.
Polymorphism is a term used for compounds that can exist in more than one crystal form, not specifically referring to the same element.
This option suggests that none of the terms apply, which is incorrect because allotropy is the correct term for this phenomenon.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Allotropy. Allotropy is the phenomenon where the same element can exist in different forms in the same physical state, such as carbon existing as graphite and diamond. This is distinct from Isomorphism, which involves compounds that share the same crystal structure but are not the same element. Polymorphism refers to the ability of a compound to crystallize in more than one form, whereas the question specifically concerns the same element in different forms. None is incorrect because the concept of allotropy directly applies to the question.

questions.text: Diamond and Graphite
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isotopes can be defined as the variants of chemical elements that possess the same number of protons and electrons, but a different number of neutrons. Both allotropes and isotopes refer to different forms of a particular chemical element. Allotropes explain the differences in molecular structures. Isotopes explain the differences in atomic structures. Diamond and graphite have differences in their molecular structures, not atomic so option A is incorrect.

Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers. The difference in arrangement of carbon atoms is due to allotropy not isomerism so option B is incorrect.

Diamond and Graphite are both allotropes of carbon. Allotropes of some chemical elements exist in two or more different forms, in the same physical state. Allotropes are different structural modifications of an element: the atoms of the element are bonded together differently. Allotropes may show differences in chemical and physical properties.
Allotropes of an element have different structures so option D in incorrect.
Graphite is very soft and has a hardness of 1 to 2 on the Mohs scale. Diamonds are the hardest known natural substance and has a hardness of 10 so option E is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Diamond and Graphite are allotropes of carbon. The term allotrope refers to one or more physical forms of a chemical element that occurs in the same physical state. Allotropes may show differences in chemical and physical properties.

questions.text: The properties which depends upon the direction are called:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: a) Isotropic properties: Isotropic properties are those that are the same in all directions. In other words, the value of the property does not vary with the direction in which it is measured. Examples of isotropic properties include density, pressure, and volume.

b) Anisotropic properties: Anisotropic properties are those that vary with direction. The value of the property changes depending on the orientation or direction in which it is measured. Examples of anisotropic properties include refractive index, thermal conductivity, and electrical conductivity.

c) Additive properties: Additive properties are those that can be combined or added together. For example, the total volume of a mixture of substances is the sum of the individual volumes of each component. This term does not specifically refer to properties that depend on direction.

d) Colligative properties: Colligative properties are properties of a solution that depend on the number of solute particles present, rather than the specific nature of the solute. These properties include vapor pressure lowering, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure.

questions.explanation: Anisotropy is the property which depends on the direction of measurement. Crystalline solids are anisotropic and they show different values of physical properties when measured along different directions.

questions.text: The properties which depend upon the direction are called _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isotropic properties refer to properties that do not depend on the direction. These properties are the same in all directions. For example, the density of a substance is an isotropic property because it remains the same regardless of the direction in which it is measured.

Anisotropy refers to properties that do depend on the direction. These properties vary depending on the direction in which they are measured. An example of an anisotropic property is the electrical conductivity of a crystal, which can differ along different crystallographic directions.

Temperature is a measure of how hot or cold something is. It is a property that helps us understand the heat energy present in a substance or the surroundings. Temperature is typically measured using a thermometer and is commonly expressed in units such as degrees Celsius or Fahrenheit. It plays a crucial role in various chemical and physical processes, including changes in states of matter.
Sublimation is the process in which a substance changes directly from a solid to a gas without passing through the liquid phase. It is not related to properties that depend on the direction.

questions.explanation: Anisotropic properties are the properties that exhibit different values when measured along with different directions. Thus anisotropy is a direction-dependent phenomenon.

questions.text: Cleavage Plane appears when a crystal is _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: False!A cleavage plane appears in a crystal when it is subjected to mechanical stress along a specific direction or plane that corresponds to a weak bonding region within the crystal lattice. This stress can cause the crystal to split or break along the plane of weakest bonding, resulting in a smooth, flat surface known as the cleavage plane.

A cleavage plane appears in a crystal when it is subjected to mechanical stress along a specific direction or plane that corresponds to a weak bonding region within the crystal lattice. This stress can cause the crystal to split or break along the plane of weakest bonding, resulting in a smooth, flat surface known as the cleavage plane.

False!A cleavage plane appears in a crystal when it is subjected to mechanical stress along a specific direction or plane that corresponds to a weak bonding region within the crystal lattice. This stress can cause the crystal to split or break along the plane of weakest bonding, resulting in a smooth, flat surface known as the cleavage plane.

False!A cleavage plane appears in a crystal when it is subjected to mechanical stress along a specific direction or plane that corresponds to a weak bonding region within the crystal lattice. This stress can cause the crystal to split or break along the plane of weakest bonding, resulting in a smooth, flat surface known as the cleavage plane.

questions.explanation: A cleavage plane appears in a crystal when it is subjected to mechanical stress along a specific direction or plane that corresponds to a weak bonding region within the crystal lattice. This stress can cause the crystal to split or break along the plane of weakest bonding, resulting in a smooth, flat surface known as the cleavage plane.

questions.text: The following are anisotropic properties except:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: a) Refractive Index: Anisotropic materials have different refractive indices in different directions. This means that light passing through the material will experience different speeds and directions of propagation depending on its polarization and the direction of travel. Therefore, refractive index can be an anisotropic property.

b) Thermal Conductivity: Anisotropic materials can conduct heat differently along different directions. For example, in a crystal, heat may be conducted more efficiently along one crystallographic direction compared to others. Therefore, thermal conductivity can be an anisotropic property.

c) Electrical Conductivity: Anisotropic materials can also conduct electricity differently along different directions. The movement of charge carriers may be more efficient in certain directions compared to others. Therefore, electrical conductivity can be an anisotropic property.

d) Melting Points: Melting point is typically not considered an anisotropic property. It is a scalar property that represents the temperature at which a substance changes from solid to liquid phase. Anisotropy is more related to directional variations in properties.

questions.explanation: d) Melting Points: Melting point is typically not considered an anisotropic property. It is a scalar property that represents the temperature at which a substance changes from solid to liquid phase. Anisotropy is more related to directional variations in properties.

questions.text: Which solids are called true solids?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Metallic solids have a structure formed by metal atoms and contain a 'sea of electrons' which allows for conductivity. However, they lack a consistent long-range order throughout the entire structure, which is necessary for a 'true solid'.
Amorphous solids do not have a long-range order in their atomic arrangement, leading to an irregular structure. This lack of order prevents them from being classified as 'true solids'.

Crystalline solids are known as 'true solids' because they have a well-ordered, repeating crystal lattice structure that extends throughout the entire solid. This gives them distinctive physical properties such as sharp melting points and defined geometric shapes.
Vitreous solids, similar to amorphous solids, lack a long-range ordered structure. They are often glass-like and do not fit the criteria of 'true solids' due to their disordered atomic arrangement.

questions.explanation: Crystalline solids are considered "true solids." They have a highly ordered and repetitive arrangement of particles extending throughout the entire solid, forming a crystal lattice structure. This arrangement results in characteristic properties such as distinct melting points and regular cleavage planes. Therefore, option c is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following is anisotropic:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Hydrogen exists as a gas, and its molecules are not organized in a crystalline structure, meaning it lacks anisotropic properties. Its properties are isotropic, meaning they are the same in all directions.
Quartz is a crystalline solid with a regular arrangement of atoms. This regularity leads to anisotropic properties, where characteristics like optical and mechanical properties vary with direction.
Water is a liquid and does not have a crystalline structure when in this state. Its molecules are randomly arranged, making it isotropic, not anisotropic.
Rubber is an amorphous solid, and its molecules are arranged randomly, which results in isotropic properties rather than anisotropic.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct.Quartz is the only anisotropic material out of the options you have given.An anisotropic material is a material whose properties are directionally dependent.This means that the properties of the material, such as its strength,stiffness, and electrical conductivity,will vary depending on the direction in which they are measured.Quartz is a crystalline material, and its crystals are arranged in a regular pattern.This regular pattern gives quartz its anisotropic properties.

questions.text: Transition temperature is shown by which of the following pairs:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Isomorphs are compounds with the same crystal structure but different chemical compositions, while polymorphs are different crystalline forms of the same compound. This pair does not exhibit a transition temperature as they are not different states of the same substance.

Polymorphs are different crystalline forms of the same compound, and allotropes are different structural forms of the same element. Both can exhibit transition temperatures as they represent different states of the same substance under specific conditions.

Isomorphs are not related to transition temperatures because they involve different compounds with similar structures, while allotropes involve different forms of the same element. This pair does not exhibit transition temperatures.
This option is incorrect because not all combinations involve transition temperatures. Only the pair 'allotrope and polymorph' does.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Allotrope and polymorph.' Polymorphs refer to different crystalline forms of the same compound, while allotropes refer to different structural forms of the same element. Both can exhibit transition temperatures because they represent different phases or forms of the same substance, which can change under specific temperature and pressure conditions. The other options involve combinations that do not transition between states of the same substance.

questions.text: Transition temperature of sulphur is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This temperature is too low for the transition between crystalline forms of sulphur.
This temperature is above the transition temperature for sulphur's crystalline changes.
At 95.5°C, sulphur transitions from rhombic to monoclinic form, making it the correct transition temperature.
This temperature is not related to any known transition of crystalline sulphur.

questions.explanation: The transition temperature of sulphur where it changes from rhombic to monoclinic form is 95.5°C. At this temperature, sulphur undergoes a structural change in its crystalline form. The other temperatures listed do not correspond to any recognized transitions in sulphur's crystalline state. For example, 13.2°C and 32.02°C are too low, and 128°C is higher than the transition temperature.

questions.text: Which one has the highest melting point and boiling point?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic crystals have high melting and boiling points due to strong electrostatic forces between oppositely charged ions. However, covalent crystals have stronger directional covalent bonds that require even more energy to break.

Molecular crystals consist of molecules held together by weak intermolecular forces such as Van der Waals forces, resulting in relatively low melting and boiling points compared to other types of crystals.

Covalent crystals are characterized by strong covalent bonds in a rigid lattice, requiring substantial energy to break these bonds. This results in very high melting and boiling points, often exceeding those of ionic crystals.

Metallic crystals have metallic bonds, which can vary in strength. While they generally have high melting and boiling points, they often do not exceed those of covalent crystals, which have stronger covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: Covalent crystals, due to the strong covalent bonds holding their structures together, typically exhibit the highest melting and boiling points among the given options. Therefore, option c is correct.

questions.text: The extension of structure in a crystalline solid is?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Crystalline solids are those in which the atoms, ions, or molecules that make up the solid exist in a regular, well-defined arrangement.
Crystalline solids have atoms arranged in a symmetrical, lattice-type geometric pattern.

Because a crystalline solid consists of repeating patterns of its components in three dimensions (a crystal lattice), we can represent the entire crystal by drawing the structure of the smallest identical units that, when stacked together, form the crystal. This basic repeating unit is called a unit cell.
Crystalline solids have a repeating pattern, in terms of the arrangement of atoms, giving the structure a Geometric appearance which is in three dimensions.

questions.explanation: Crystalline solids have a repeating pattern, in terms of the arrangement of atoms, giving the structure a geometric appearance, which is in three dimaensions.

questions.text: When an element occurs in different physical forms it is called _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Anisotropy refers to the dependence of a material's properties on direction.
Isomorphism refers to the ability of two or more compounds to have the same crystal structure.
Polymorphism refers to the ability of a compound to exist in different crystal forms, but it's typically used to describe molecules, not elements.

Allotropy refers to the ability of an element to exist in different physical forms, often with different crystal structures or molecular arrangements. This can result in different properties, such as density, hardness, or color. Examples of allotropes include:- Carbon: diamond, graphite, and fullerenes- Sulfur: orthorhombic, monoclinic, and rhombic forms- Phosphorus: white, black, and red forms

questions.explanation: Allotropy refers to the ability of an element to exist in different physical forms, often with different crystal structures or molecular arrangements. This can result in different properties, such as density, hardness, or color. Examples of allotropes include:- Carbon: diamond, graphite, and fullerenes- Sulfur: orthorhombic, monoclinic, and rhombic forms- Phosphorus: white, black, and red forms

questions.text: The temperature at which one crystalline form changes to another is called _ temperature.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: The critical temperature refers to the temperature above which a substance cannot exist as a liquid, regardless of the pressure. This is commonly seen in the context of supercritical fluids.

The absolute temperature is a temperature scale that is based on absolute zero, the theoretical temperature at which all matter would have zero thermal energy. This temperature scale is commonly used in scientific applications.

At the 'Transition temperature' a material changes from an allotropic crystal state to another. To be more specific, at the 'Transition temperature', two crystalline forms of a substance can co-exist in equilibrium.

options.text: None of these options is correct
options.explanation: "None of these options is correct" is also incorrect as the correct answer is (C) Transition.

questions.explanation: Option A: The critical temperature refers to the temperature above which a substance cannot exist as a liquid, regardless of the pressure. This is commonly seen in the context of supercritical fluids.Option B: The absolute temperature is a temperature scale that is based on absolute zero, the theoretical temperature at which all matter would have zero thermal energy. This temperature scale is commonly used in scientific applications.Option C: At the 'Transition temperature' a material changes from one allotropic crystal state to another. To be more specific, at the 'Transition temperature', two crystalline forms of a substance can co-exist in equilibrium.Option D: "None of these options is correct" is also incorrect as the correct answer is (C) Transition.

questions.text: Which type of movement is shown by the atoms of the solid?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because, unlike solids, liquids have translational energy. The liquid molecules can move past each other.

This option is correct because the particles vibrate back and forth but remain in their fixed positions. As a solid is heated, the particles vibrate faster and faster until they have enough energy to break away from their fixed positions.
This option is incorrect because only gas and liquids have rotational motion.
This option is incorrect because only liquids show linear motion. After all, gases have haphazard motion.

questions.explanation: The correct option is B. Solid has a fixed shape and a fixed volume because the particles can move only a little. The particles vibrate back and forth but remain in their fixed positions. As a solid is heated, the particles vibrate faster and faster until they have enough energy to break away from their fixed positions.

questions.text: To conduct electricity through Ionic Solids, the ions should be:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: In the context of ionic solids, "excited" usually refers to the state of an electron in an atom or ion that has absorbed energy and moved to a higher energy level. While the concept of electronic excitation is essential in various chemical and physical processes, it is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through ionic solids.

"Energized" generally means that a system or particle has gained energy. In the case of ionic solids, energy is indeed required to initiate the conduction of electricity, but it is not the ions themselves that need to be energized. Instead, the energy typically comes from an external source like an electric field, which allows the ions to move and conduct electricity.

The correct answer is "c) Free." For a material to conduct electricity, it must have mobile charge carriers. In ionic solids, these mobile charge carriers are free ions, which can move under the influence of an electric field. When an ionic solid is dissolved in water or melted, the ions become free and can move, resulting in electrical conductivity.

While the ions in an ionic solid do vibrate and have thermal motion, this random motion is not directly related to the conduction of electricity through the solid. Electrical conductivity in ionic solids primarily depends on the availability of free ions that can move in response to an electric field.

questions.explanation: To conduct energy through Ionic Solids, the ions should be free to move. Ionic compounds conduct electricity as liquids or when in solution since the ions are free to move. Ionic compounds do not conduct electricity in a solid state since the ions are held in place and can not move.

questions.text: Brittleness of ionic solids is due to the fact that ions arrange themselves in a way to _ each other.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Ionic solids are held together by the attraction between positively and negatively charged ions. However, this attraction alone does not explain the brittleness of ionic solids.

This statement is partially true because applying compressive forces to an ionic solid can cause the ions to move closer together, which can increase the strength of the electrostatic forces holding the solid together. However, this does not explain why ionic solids are brittle.

To an ionic compound when a force is applied, like charges in the lattice come together due to the force applied and repel each other, breaking the compound hence ionic compounds are brittle, as like charges repel.
This option is also incorrect. Overlapping of ions would indicate a breakdown in the crystalline structure of an ionic solid, and would not contribute to its brittleness.

questions.explanation: To an ionic compound when a force is applied, like charges in the lattice come together due to the force applied and repel each other, breaking the compound hence ionic compounds are brittle, as like charges repel.

questions.text: The bond length of the Cl-Cl bond in gaseous chlorine is approximately 199pm. In solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This option is wrong because, in solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is not 143 pm.

In solid iodine, the bond distance between I-I is 271m. The reason is simple. Iodine is two rows down, so, between chlorine and iodine, you have the subshells 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, and finally 5p. It is a lot of electrons, which repel each other. The fact that iodine is solid has not much significance: the covalent interactions between iodine atoms are far stronger than the dispersion (London) intermolecular forces; the latter are nevertheless far stronger than in the case of chlorine because a huge number of electrons in a large space means greater polarizability, which makes it possible for iodine to be in a condensed state at room temperature. The reason why it is solid is due to the regular shape of I2 molecules (they are diatomic ones), which makes it easy to pack many molecules to yield a solid structure. Anyway, iodine sublimates very easily.
This option is wrong because, in solid iodine, the I-I bond distance is not 227pm.
This option is wrong because in solid iodine the I-I bond distance is not 166pm.

questions.explanation: Option: B In solid iodine, the bond distance between I-I is 271m. The reason is simple. Iodine is two rows down, so, between chlorine and iodine, you have the subshells 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, and finally 5p. It is a lot of electrons, which repel each other. The fact that iodine is solid has not much significance: the covalent interactions between iodine atoms are far stronger than the dispersion (London) intermolecular forces; the latter are nevertheless far stronger than in the case of chlorine because a huge number of electrons in a large space means greater polarizability, which makes it possible for iodine to be in a condensed state at room temperature. The reason why it is solid is due to the regular shape of I2 molecules (they are diatomic ones), which makes it easy to pack many molecules to yield a solid structure. Anyway, iodine sublimates very easily.

questions.text: Which of the following solids shows hydrogen bonding?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: "Ionic" is incorrect because ionic solids are composed of ions held together by ionic bonds, which are electrostatic attractions between positively and negatively charged ions.

"Metallic" is incorrect because metallic solids are composed of metal atoms held together by metallic bonds, which involve the delocalization of electrons throughout the solid lattice. Metallic bonding does not involve hydrogen bonding.

"Covalent" is incorrect because covalent solids are composed of atoms held together by covalent bonds, which involve the sharing of electrons between atoms. While covalent compounds can have strong bonds, they do not typically exhibit hydrogen bonding.
The correct answer is "Molecular." Solids that exhibit hydrogen bonding are typically molecular solids.

questions.explanation: Ionic solids are crystals held together by ionic bonding between oppositely charged anions and cations. Metals are solids held together by metallic bonding, i.e., electrostatic forces of attraction between positive cations and a negative sea of delocalized electrons. Covalent solids (like diamonds) are held together by covalent bonds between atoms. Molecular solids are held together by intermolecular forces of attraction, including hydrogen bonding and Vander Waals.

questions.text: Crystalline Solids can be converted into amorphous solids by _.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: Sublimation is the process in which a solid transforms into a gas phase without first melting to form a liquid phase while the conversion of crystalline to Amorphous solid is a solid-to-solid change.
Evaporation is the process by which a liquid turns into a gas. It takes place in liquids.
Many crystalline solids can be changed into amorphous solids by melting and then cooling the molten mass rapidly. In this way, constituent particles do not find time to arrange themselves.
Hammering does not result in an amorphous solid.

questions.explanation: Many crystalline solids can be changed into amorphous solids by melting and then cooling the molten mass rapidly. In this way, constituent particles do not find time to arrange themselves.
